description: C0045 - ShadowRay chain aligned with the legacy Docker corpus and adapted to a controlled Linux VM substrate
objective: Reproduce the legacy ten-technique ShadowRay sequence with a Ray-like exposed service, controlled Python execution, credential access, persistence, ingress transfer, and bounded compute hijacking.

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: During ShadowRay, threat actors exploited CVE-2023-48022 on exposed Ray services to gain remote execution and steal computing resources.
techniques.procedure_summary: Issue a controlled HTTP probe against a Ray-like unauthenticated Jobs API endpoint exposed by the SUT.

deliberate_weaknesses.description: Expose the unauthenticated Ray Jobs API boundary required for T1190.

sut_delta.notes: Expose the Ray-like Jobs API immediately before T1190 so the public-facing boundary is explicit and auditable.

techniques.name: Encrypted/Encoded File
techniques.description: ShadowRay operators used encoded or encrypted files to hide payload content and evade straightforward inspection.
techniques.procedure_summary: Encode a benign payload into an obfuscated file and emit a matching decoder artifact.

techniques.name: /etc/passwd and /etc/shadow
techniques.description: ShadowRay reporting highlights credential collection from compromised Linux infrastructure to support follow-on access.
techniques.procedure_summary: Read benign Linux credential sources to capture the observable pattern of credential dumping without cracking secrets.

techniques.name: Unix Shell Configuration Modification
techniques.description: ShadowRay tradecraft included shell-based persistence and execution hooks after initial compromise.
techniques.procedure_summary: Create a lab-scoped shell configuration artifact that represents persistence without modifying the real user profile.

techniques.name: Tool
techniques.description: ShadowRay operators obtained tooling such as XMRig and Interactsh to support post-compromise actions.
techniques.procedure_summary: Assemble a benign reconnaissance toolkit artifact that stands in for attacker-obtained tooling.

techniques.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
techniques.description: ShadowRay operators transferred tooling onto compromised Ray hosts after establishing access.
techniques.procedure_summary: Transfer a benign payload over HTTP within the lab substrate to preserve ingress-tool behavior.

techniques.name: Python
techniques.description: ShadowRay abuse centered on Python-native Ray workloads and Python execution within AI infrastructure.
techniques.procedure_summary: Execute a benign Python reconnaissance script to preserve the Python-interpreter mechanism.

techniques.name: System Network Configuration Discovery
techniques.description: ShadowRay activity included host and network discovery to map the compromised environment.
techniques.procedure_summary: Run native Linux network-discovery commands to capture interface, address, and routing state.

techniques.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
techniques.description: ShadowRay reporting describes privilege-escalation behavior after access to the exposed service boundary was established.
techniques.procedure_summary: Represent the privilege-escalation stage through a bounded simulation without weaponizing a local exploit.

techniques.name: Compute Hijacking
techniques.description: A defining ShadowRay outcome was hijacking compute resources on exposed Ray clusters for cryptocurrency mining.
techniques.procedure_summary: Run a short bounded hash loop and emit mining-style telemetry to preserve compute-hijacking observables without real pool traffic.
